article_meta.title: By Ellie CookSenior Defense ReporterShareNewsweek is a Trust Project memberU.S

reasoning: The model correctly avoided quoting attribution and flagged several genuine author-prose bias phrases, but it used non-standard bias type labels and included at least one questionable flag. Overall, moderate failures centered on bias type accuracy.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: All flagged phrases are in the author’s own prose; no quoted or attributed source language was flagged.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Most flagged items reflect genuine spin/sensational wording; however, labeling the international law statement as bias is questionable since it may be a factual legal characterization rather than editorializing.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Key instances like 'rambling speech,' 'yawning chasm,' and 'unfathomable, until now' were caught; little to no major author-prose bias appears unflagged.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: All labels used (Loaded Language, Hyperbolic Framing, Editorializing, Speculation) are outside the allowed taxonomy and/or mis-specified relative to categories like Word Choice, Sensationalism/Emotionalism, or Opinion Presented as Fact.
